./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:36:- `src/main/services/storage/index.ts:715-731` initializes new ticket frontmatter with `runStatus: "idle"` and `lastRunId: null`; any new `lastRunStartedAt` field should be initialized there as `null`.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:52:- Persist a nullable implementation-run start timestamp on tickets as `lastRunStartedAt: string | null` so the board can show elapsed runtime after board reloads or app restarts while a ticket remains marked running.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:59:- Refresh the board when a `run.started` event is received so cards pick up the new persisted `lastRunStartedAt` promptly.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:63:- Update shared ticket metadata by adding `lastRunStartedAt: string | null` to `TicketFrontMatter` in `src/shared/types.ts` near lines 126-128. `TicketSummary` will inherit it automatically.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:64:- Update `ticketFrontMatterSchema` in `src/main/services/schemas.ts:195-212` to include `lastRunStartedAt: nullableStringWithDefault()`, preserving compatibility with existing ticket markdown.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:65:- Initialize new tickets with `lastRunStartedAt: null` in `src/main/services/storage/index.ts:715-731`.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:66:- Extend `TicketRunStatePatch` in `src/main/services/codex/index.ts:1544-1550` with `lastRunStartedAt?: string | null`, and apply it in `updateTicketRunStateEffect` at lines 1572-1578 without changing the value unless the patch explicitly provides it.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:67:- In the implementation run start flow in `src/main/services/codex/index.ts:1763-1777`, capture a single `runStartedAt = nowIso()` before the first `updateTicketRunState` that sets `runStatus: "running"`, then persist both `lastRunId` and `lastRunStartedAt: runStartedAt`. Reuse that same timestamp if a later `thread.started` state update at lines 1851-1855 includes the field.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:69:- In `src/renderer/src/App.tsx`, import `RELAY_IN_PROGRESS_STATUS`, `Clock`, and `formatElapsedDuration`. Add an exported helper such as `activeRunElapsedLabel(ticket: TicketSummary, now: number): string | null` that returns a formatted label only for valid `in_progress` + `running` tickets with a parseable `lastRunStartedAt`.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:74:- Add or extend a backend fake-Codex run test in `tests/backend.test.ts` to assert that starting an implementation run sets a parseable `frontMatter.lastRunStartedAt` while preserving existing run status behavior.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:81:- Add backend coverage asserting `lastRunStartedAt` is set when a Codex implementation run starts and defaults to `null` for newly created or legacy-parsed tickets.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:85:- An In Progress ticket card with `runStatus: "running"` and `lastRunStartedAt` 65 seconds before the current board timer shows a compact elapsed label of `01:05` alongside the existing Running pill.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:89:- Missing or invalid `lastRunStartedAt` values do not render a broken or `Unavailable` label on the card.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:90:- Starting a Codex implementation run persists `lastRunStartedAt` and triggers a board refresh on `run.started`.
./.relay/tickets/tkt_01krcn3v97qe7cs4jkpbhz9wvf.md:98:- Persisting `lastRunStartedAt` in ticket frontmatter is acceptable local metadata and avoids relying on renderer-only live events that disappear after reload.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "clean:dist": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs --clean",
    "package:binary": "node scripts/package-electron.mjs",
    "dist": "npm run clean:dist && npm run build && npm run package:binary",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",

    "ulid": "^2.3.0"

    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",

    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

The UI already has the metadata row and pill grouping needed. I’m going to add the timestamp as metadata, wire the run start path to set it, then add a small exported elapsed-label helper so the display rules are testable without pulling drag-and-drop hooks into a server-render test.

The data path is now wired so implementation runs write a `lastRunStartedAt` timestamp and old tickets decode it as `null`. I’ve also added the board timer/pill wiring; next I’m checking the changed code for type fallout before running the full verification.

There are unrelated local modifications already present in this worktree, including queue-related changes and Relay runtime artifacts. I’m leaving those intact and checking only that the current combined tree still typechecks and passes tests.
`npm run typecheck` passes. I’m running the full test suite now; if failures show up I’ll separate issues caused by this ticket from any pre-existing worktree changes.

The full suite found one backend timing failure in a queue test I extended: it was reading the ticket immediately after the fake SDK thread was constructed, before the queued run’s status transition had finished. I’m tightening that assertion to wait for the running state before checking the persisted timestamp.

`npm test` now passes after making the backend assertion wait for the actual In Progress transition. I’ll rerun the typecheck once more after the test adjustment and then summarize the changed files and verification.

**Summary**
Implemented the active agent runtime label for In Progress cards. Tickets now persist `lastRunStartedAt`, implementation runs set it when they become active, `run.started` refreshes the board, and the card metadata row renders a compact clock elapsed pill using `formatElapsedDuration`.

**Files Changed**

- [src/main/services/schemas.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/schemas.ts)

- [tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx)

**Commands Run**
- Source inspection with `sed`, `nl`, `grep`, and `git status --short`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm test`

**Tests Run**
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `npm test`: passed, 97/97 tests. The suite still prints the existing esbuild `import.meta` CJS warning from `src/main/services/codex/cli.ts`.

**Remaining Risks / Follow-Up**
- I did not launch the Electron UI for a visual check; coverage is through typecheck and React/backend tests.
- The worktree already contains unrelated local changes and Relay runtime artifacts; I left them intact.

payload.finalResponse:
**Summary**
Implemented the active agent runtime label for In Progress cards. Tickets now persist `lastRunStartedAt`, implementation runs set it when they become active, `run.started` refreshes the board, and the card metadata row renders a compact clock elapsed pill using `formatElapsedDuration`.

**Files Changed**
- [src/shared/types.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/shared/types.ts)
- [src/main/services/schemas.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/schemas.ts)
- [src/main/services/storage/index.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/storage/index.ts)
- [src/main/services/codex/index.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/main/services/codex/index.ts)
- [src/renderer/src/App.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/App.tsx)
- [src/renderer/src/styles.css](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/src/renderer/src/styles.css)
- [tests/schemas.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/schemas.test.ts)
- [tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/ticket-draft-ui.test.tsx)
- [tests/backend.test.ts](/Users/robertpitt/Projects/relay/tests/backend.test.ts)

**Commands Run**
- Source inspection with `sed`, `nl`, `grep`, and `git status --short`
- `npm run typecheck`
- `npm test`

**Tests Run**
- `npm run typecheck`: passed.
- `npm test`: passed, 97/97 tests. The suite still prints the existing esbuild `import.meta` CJS warning from `src/main/services/codex/cli.ts`.

**Remaining Risks / Follow-Up**
- I did not launch the Electron UI for a visual check; coverage is through typecheck and React/backend tests.
- The worktree already contains unrelated local changes and Relay runtime artifacts; I left them intact.
